Senior Business Analyst

Role Overview

Senior Business Analyst with at least 5 years of experience in IT and technology-driven projects. The role will be responsible for understanding client requirements, designing appropriate solutions, preparing functional documentation, and managing client engagements throughout the project lifecycle.

Key Responsibilities

  • Conduct client discussions, workshops, and requirement-gathering sessions.
  • Analyse business processes and identify opportunities for process improvement and optimization.
  • Design practical technology solutions aligned with business requirements.
  • Prepare process maps, BRDs, functional specifications, workflows, user stories, and other project documentation.
  • Manage client expectations, communication, scope, risks, and project priorities.
  • Coordinate with technical, functional, and project teams to ensure successful delivery.
  • Support solutioning across ERP, AI, automation, business intelligence, databases, and other technology platforms.
  • Participate in project planning, UAT, implementation, training, and post-go-live support.
  • Identify edge cases, dependencies, gaps, and potential risks before development.
  • Support proposal preparation, effort estimation, presentations, and solution demonstrations.

Experience and Qualifications

  • Minimum 5 years of experience as a Business Analyst, Functional Consultant, Solution Consultant, or similar role.
  • Strong experience in IT solutions, ERP systems, process mapping, and process optimization.
  • Working knowledge of AI solutions, automation, databases, APIs, and system integrations.
  • Basic understanding of finance, accounting processes, and financial reporting.
  • Experience in project management and coordinating with multiple stakeholders.
  • Strong documentation, presentation, and client-management skills.

Key Competencies

  • Excellent problem-solving and critical-thinking abilities.
  • Ability to understand complex business requirements and convert them into practical solutions.
  • Strong attention to detail and structured documentation.
  • Adaptable and capable of handling new requirements, changing priorities, and unexpected edge cases.
  • Strong communication, stakeholder-management, and negotiation skills.
  • Self-driven, organized, and capable of managing multiple projects simultaneously.

Contact: Share your updated resume at mitty.j@difinitydigital.com
summary: Senior Business Analyst with 5+ years experience required to analyze client needs, design IT solutions, and manage project lifecycles. Must have expertise in ERP, AI, and automation.

About the Role

We are seeking a qualified capital/finance professional to lead the capital and control workstream for new regulated insurance entities established by the Group — including DFSA, Cayman, and Lloyd’s structures. The primary focus is on new entity establishment: defining the capital structure, building the financial control framework, and establishing the regulatory reporting infrastructure from inception. Once operational, you will support ongoing financial and regulatory requirements, alongside existing entities and reinsurance programs. Supervision of a junior analyst is also required. This is a build-focused role for someone comfortable creating systems from scratch, not just managing steady-state reporting.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead financial establishment of new regulated entities (DFSA, Cayman, Lloyd’s) — defining opening balance sheets, capital structures, and financial control frameworks.
  • Build capital adequacy and solvency models for new entities to meet regulatory requirements; maintain and update as entities grow.
  • Establish regulatory reporting frameworks for new jurisdictions (e.g., DFSA, Cayman, Lloyd’s), including designing reporting packs, setting calendars, and producing initial returns.
  • Build three-statement financial models and entity-level profit/loss structures for new entities.
  • Establish reinsurance program accounting structures (quota share, excess of loss, ceding commission) and support existing programs.
  • Produce monthly management accounts and reporting packs for new entities; support ongoing production for existing entities.
  • Reconcile intercompany balances across group entities.
  • Manage the Group’s Funds at Lloyd’s (FAL) position for Syndicate in a Box (SIAB), including capital setting processes, FAL composition, and capital calls.
  • Run capital adequacy stress testing and scenario analysis across regulated entities to assess headroom above SCR/MCR thresholds.
  • Manage collateral arrangements (letters of credit, trust accounts) for reinsurance programs.
  • Own capital waterfall and inter-entity funding mechanics, ensuring compliance with approved limits.
  • Track reinsurance counterparty credit exposure, maintaining registers and flagging risks.
  • Produce quarterly board-level capital reporting packs (capital dashboard, solvency headroom, FAL position, stress test outcomes).
  • Support existing entities and reinsurance programs with financial control, reporting, or capital queries.
  • Supervise and direct the Capital & Structuring Analyst.

Preferred Skills

  • Qualified or finalist accountant (ACCA, CIMA, ACA, or equivalent).
  • Experience with capital adequacy, solvency reporting, and regulatory returns in insurance/reinsurance contexts.
  • Hands-on financial modeling experience in insurance/reinsurance.
  • Understanding of reinsurance accounting (quota share, excess of loss) and collateral management.
  • Demonstrable experience setting up financial/reporting infrastructure for regulated entities in DFSA/DIFC, Cayman, or Lloyd’s.
  • Familiarity with Lloyd’s capital processes (FAL management, ECM/SBF cycle, SIAB structures).
  • Strong Xero and advanced Excel/modeling skills.
  • Ability to work independently.
summary: Lead capital and control workstreams for new regulated insurance entities, establishing financial structures and regulatory reporting. Requires expertise in capital adequacy, financial modeling, and reinsurance accounting.
